metadata.notes: Asynchronous notifications to relevant actors after changes are saved to the system.

inputs.vague: A customer needs to update the details of a SuperFrog appearance request she already submitted, as long as it is still in Pending, Approved, or Assigned status. She provides the request identifier, reviews the current information, and makes the necessary changes. The system validates the edits, shows any warnings, and asks her to confirm against the TCU Spirit Appearance terms before saving. Relevant parties such as the Spirit Director and assigned SuperFrog Student are notified of the changes.
inputs.detailed:
Primary Actor:
Customer
Secondary Actors:
Spirit Director, SuperFrog Student
Trigger:
The Customer indicates to edit a submitted SuperFrog appearance request.
Description:
The Customer wants to edit a submitted SuperFrog request, so that she can provide the most recent SuperFrog request information to the TCU Spirit Team.
Preconditions:
PRE-1. A request of the Customer has been submitted to the System and is in either “Pending” or “Approved” or “Assigned” status. See BR-15.
Postconditions:
POST-1. Changes made to the request are stored in the System.
Main Success Scenario:
The Customer indicates to change the details of an existing SuperFrog appearance request. The Customer provides the unique identifier of the request to the System.
The System retrieves the specified request and displays it to the Customer.
The Customer chooses to change the details of this request.
The System asks the Customer to make changes to this request where allowed according to the “Details” defined in the Associated Information and the “Security/access concerns” defined in the Business Rules of this use case.
The Customer makes changes to this request until she confirms that she has finished changing.
The System validates the Customer’s changes and alerts warning messages according to the “Details” defined in the Associated Information of this use case.
The Customer acknowledges the warnings and chooses to continue.
The System displays the updated details of this request, the terms & conditions of a TCU Spirit Appearance, and alerts the Customer to confirm the change.
The Customer either agrees to the terms & conditions of a TCU Spirit Appearance and confirms the change (continues the normal flow) or chooses to continue to change the details (returns to step 5).
The System saves the changes, carries out the effect of change according to the “Details” defined in the Associated Information of this use case, and informs the Customer that this request has been changed.
The System notifies relevant actors about the change of request according to the “Notification” defined in the Associated Information of this use case.
Use case ends.
Extensions:
6a. Input validation rule violation:
6a1. The System alerts the Customer that an input validation rule is violated and displays the nature and location of the error.
6a2. The Customer corrects the mistake and returns to step 6 of the normal flow.

groundTruth.name: Edit SuperFrog Appearance Request
groundTruth.summary: The Customer wants to edit a submitted SuperFrog request, so that she can provide the most recent SuperFrog request information to the TCU Spirit Team.
preconditions: A request of the Customer has been submitted to the System and is in either “Pending” or “Approved” or “Assigned” status.

steps.description: Indicates to change the details of an existing SuperFrog appearance request.
Provides the unique identifier of the request to the System.
Retrieves the specified request and displays it to the Customer.
Chooses to change the details of this request.
Asks the Customer to make changes to this request where allowed.
Validates the Customer’s changes and alerts warning messages.
Acknowledges the warnings and chooses to continue.
Displays the updated details of this request, the terms & conditions of a TCU Spirit Appearance, and alerts the Customer to confirm the change.
Agrees to the terms & conditions of a TCU Spirit Appearance and confirms the change.
Saves the changes, carries out the effect of change, and informs the Customer that this request has been changed.
Notifies relevant actors about the change of request.
Alerts the Customer that an input validation rule is violated and displays the nature and location of the error.
Corrects the mistake and returns to step 6 of the normal flow.
